Grafana Labs的目标是提供一种更好的软件开发方式，并已完成了一轮5000万美元的B系列融资，以更接近其目标。
在现有投资者LightSpeed Venture Partners的牵头下，这轮超额认购还包括来自另一家现有支持者Lead Edge Capital的额外资金。
根据Crunchbase的数据，包括这笔新投资在内，总部位于纽约的Grafana自2015年以来总共筹集了7520万美元，其中包括该公司2019年10月的首轮融资，当时它获得了2400万美元。LightSpeed和Lead Edge也在那轮融资中进行了投资。
Grafana实验室与使用其Grafana开源软件平台的组织合作，以可视化其指标、日志和跟踪数据，无论数据位于何处。除了开源平台，该公司还有两个商业产品，Grafana Enterprise和Grafana Cloud。
Grafana Labs的联合创始人兼首席执行官Raj dut告诉Crunchbase News，在提高A系列之后，Grafana专注于建立其销售和营销团队-其中包括一些战略招聘，如Doug Hanna担任首席运营官-Raj dut。
此外，Grafana创建了Grafana Accelerator计划，为处于早期阶段的公司提供赠款、股权融资和开发商准入，以及在更广泛的Grafana生态系统中开发的附属项目。
达特说：“我们希望在研发方面大幅加速，并雇佣大量工程师。”
Grafana目前有超过55万个活跃安装，该公司拥有1000多个客户。达特说，该公司将为其两种商业产品增加能力。
他补充说：“我们会专注於产品创新，以及培育和发展社区。”我们将把大部分精力花在开发开源软件上，并建立具有度量、日志和跟踪功能的能力。“。
开源软件继续获得吸引力。三年前，我们对此进行了更深入的研究，指出仅2017年一季度，就有13笔美元交易的投资额超过3.71亿美元。
根据CB Insight的市场规模工具，开源服务行业已经得到了超过3000万开发者的贡献，预计到2022年将达到近330亿美元的价值。
该领域的重量级人物包括GitLab和被微软收购的GitHub。在过去的三个月里，我们还报道了其他几家获得该行业工作资金的初创公司，包括：
Abacus.AI，开源的人工智能工具，面向没有数据科学或AI团队的客户。它完成了1300万美元的首轮融资。
Streamlitt的开源应用程序框架筹集了2100万美元的首轮融资。
Yuabyte为开源分布式结构化查询语言数据库YuabyteDB获得了超额认购的B系列资金3000万美元。
Almanac是一个基于云的平台，为专业人士提供创建、协作和共享开源工作文档的平台，该公司宣布了一轮900万美元的种子融资。
note_en:
Grafana Labs aims to provide a better way to develop software and has closed on a $50 million round of Series B funding to get closer to its goal.
  Led by existing investor  Lightspeed Venture Partners, the oversubscribed round also included additional funding from another existing backer,  Lead Edge Capital.
 Including the new investment, New York-based Grafana has raised a total of $75.2 million since 2015, which includes the company’s October 2019  Series A round, where it secured $24 million, according to Crunchbase data. Both Lightspeed and Lead Edge also invested in that round.
 Grafana Labs works with organizations using its Grafana open-source software platform for visualizing their metrics, logs and traces data, wherever it lives. In addition to the open source platform, the company has two commercial products, Grafana Enterprise and Grafana Cloud.
  After raising its Series A, Grafana focused on building out its sales and marketing teams— which included some strategic hires such as  Doug Hanna as COO— Raj Dutt, co-founder and CEO of Grafana Labs, told Crunchbase News.
   In addition, Grafana created the Grafana Accelerator Program to offer grants, equity financing and developer access for early-stage companies, and side projects developing in the wider Grafana ecosystem.
 “We want to dramatically accelerate in research and development and hire a lot of engineers,” Dutt said.
 There are now more than 550,000 active installations of Grafana, and the company has more than 1,000 customers. The company will add capabilities to both of its commercial offerings, Dutt said.
 “We will focus on product innovation and nurturing and growing the community,” he added. “We will spend most of our efforts on developing the open-source software and building out capabilities with the metrics, logs and traces.”
  Open-source software continues to gain traction. Three years ago,  we went more in-depth on the subject, noting that in the first quarter of 2017 alone, more than $371 million was invested in 13 U.S. dollar-denominated deals.
 The  open-source services industry is already being contributed to by more than 30 million developers and is expected to reach nearly $33 billion in value by 2022, according to  CB Insights’ Market Sizing tool.
 Heavy hitters in the space include  GitLab and  GitHub, which was  acquired by  Microsoft. We’ve also reported on several other startups in the past three months that received funding for their work in the sector, including:
 Abacus.AI, open sourcing an artificial intelligence tool for customers who don’t have a data science or AI team. It closed on a  $13 million Series A funding.
   Streamlit’s open-source app framework raised a  $21 million Series A round of funding.
 Yugabyte secured  $30 million in an oversubscribed Series B funding for YugabyteDB, an open-source distributed Structured Query Language database.
 Almanac, a cloud-based platform for professionals to create, collaborate and share open-source work documents, announced a  $9 million seed round of funding.
title: Grafana在B系列融资中筹集5000万美元
title_en: Grafana raises $50M in Series B funding

note:
让我们正确看待这些数字。特斯拉是一家汽车制造商，2019年交付了36.75万辆汽车，目标是在2020年超过50万辆大关，其市值超过了美国三大汽车制造商通用汽车(GM)、福特(Ford)和菲亚特克莱斯勒(Fiat Chrysler)的市值总和。打个比方，特斯拉现在的价值是这些公司总和的三倍半。
虽然特斯拉预计2020年将交付超过50万辆汽车-请记住，这不是一个让人嗤之以鼻的数字，但这是一个实质性的数字-福特仅在美国就实现了2019年241万辆的销量。同年，菲亚特克莱斯勒(Fiat Chrysler)在美国售出了220万辆汽车，通用汽车管理着289万辆。特斯拉的价值是它们总和的几倍。
特斯拉股价今天上涨超过11.2%，收于1,835.64美元，似乎没有任何根本原因。根据市场观察(MarketWatch)的数据，韦德布什分析师丹·艾夫斯(Dan Ives)今天显然对特斯拉持肯定态度，但这只值这么多钱。当然不是几百亿美元。
这里的故事是这家著名电动汽车公司的估值势头，该公司始于今年春天，自特斯拉(Tesla)最近宣布5比1的股票拆分计划以来，估值势头一直在加速。
就像一个小小的雪球变成了雪崩，这种变化被证明是令人震惊的。想想两年前特斯拉在哪里。首席执行官埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)刚刚在推特上表示，他已经“获得了资金”，并正在考虑以每股420美元的价格将特斯拉私有化。
马斯克后来会走回头路，特斯拉将继续公开上市。但随后的推文和泄露的电子邮件引起了美国证券交易委员会(U.S.Securities and Exchange Commission)的注意，该委员会后来指控马斯克犯有证券欺诈罪。双方在不承认不当行为的情况下达成了和解。根据和解协议，特斯拉同意增加两名独立董事，马斯克将辞去董事长一职，任期三年。
股价一路飙升-先涨后跌，就像波动较大的股票想要做的那样。特斯拉股价在2019年8月创下52周低点211美元。自那以来，该股已上涨约770%。
这是一个正在流传的金融笑话，每当一家公司的股价出现奇怪和令人费解的变化时，这一举动肯定是基于其未来现金流不断变化的现值。今天就是这种情况，在没有重大消息的情况下，特斯拉的股价大幅上涨。
当然，此举是基于其未来现金流不断变化的现值。
撇开笑话不谈，用你喜欢的任何正常财务指标衡量，特斯拉的股票都不是可笑的超买。例如，根据YCharts的数据，从价格/销售额来看，特斯拉的市盈率约为其收入的13.7倍。通用汽车？营收倍数为0.38倍。按价格/销售额计算，丰田的市值远远高于通用汽车，管理市盈率为0.77倍，约为通用汽车的两倍。
但这只是特斯拉收入倍数的个位数百分比。丰田的市净率为0.98倍。特斯拉的是34.6倍，同样依赖于YCharts的数据。
情况可能是这样的，特斯拉将超越竞争对手，确保电动汽车市场的头把交椅，运营电动汽车展会十年，并在股息和回购方面向股东大肆宣传。至少今天它的股票是这样定价的。如果没有达到投资者的预期，预计会在某个时候出现一些回调。也许再过几天，它就会无缘无故地增加10%。
你知道，当其未来现金流的现值升值十分之一时，就没有消息了。
有趣的是，柯尔斯滕和亚历克斯今天的样子是这样的，他们试图揭开特斯拉股价上涨的原因：
note_en:
Let’s put these numbers in perspective.  Tesla,   an automaker that delivered 367,500 vehicles in 2019 and is aiming to exceed the 500,000-mark in 2020, is worth more than the combined market valuations of America’s Big Three automakers: GM, Ford and Fiat Chrysler. Strike that, Tesla is now worth more than those companies  combined by a factor of three and a half.
 And while Tesla expects to deliver more than a half-million cars in 2020 — not a number to scoff at, mind, it’s material — Ford managed 2019 sales of 2.41 million in the U.S. alone. Fiat Chrysler sold 2.2 million cars in the U.S. during the same year, and GM managed 2.89 million. And Tesla is worth a multiple of their aggregate.
   There doesn’t appear to be any fundamental reason why Tesla’s shares rose more than 11.2% today to close at $1,835.64. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives apparently had a positive call today about Tesla, according  to MarketWatch, but that’s only worth so much. Surely not a few dozen billion dollars.
 The story here is valuation momentum at the well-known EV company that started this spring and has accelerated since  Tesla recently announced plans for a 5 for 1 stock split.
 Like a tiny snowball that has turned into an avalanche, the change is proving stunning. Consider where Tesla was two years ago. CEO Elon Musk had just tweeted that he had “funding secured” and was considering taking Tesla private at $420 a share.
 Musk would later backtrack and Tesla would remain public. But the tweets and leaked emails that followed got the attention of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, which later accused Musk of securities fraud. The parties reached a settlement without admitting wrongdoing. Under the settlement, Tesla agreed to add two independent directors and Musk would step down as chairman for three years.
 The shares bopped along — spiking and then dropping, as volatile stocks are want to do. Tesla shares reached a 52-week low of $211 in August 2019. The stock has risen around 770% since.
  It’s a running finance joke that whenever a company’s share price does something odd and inexplicable that the move was  certainly based on the changing present value of its future cash flows. Such is the case today, when Tesla shares jumped sharply sans material news.
 Surely the move was based on the changing present value of its future cash flows.
 Jokes aside, Tesla shares are not comically overbought, measured using any normal financial metric you prefer. On a price/sales basis, for example, Tesla is trading at a multiple of around 13.7x its revenue, according to  YCharts data. GM? A 0.38x revenue multiple. Toyota is worth much more than GM in price/sales terms, managing a 0.77x multiple, or about double.
 But that’s a single-digit  percentage of the revenue multiple that Tesla commands. Toyota’s price/book ratio of 0.98x. Tesla’s is 34.6x, again leaning on YCharts data.
 It may be the case that Tesla is set to race past its rivals, secure the top position in the electric car market, run the EV show for a decade and shower its shareholders in dividends and buybacks. That’s how its stock priced today, at least. If it falls short of that investor expectation, expect some pullback at some point. And perhaps some more days when it just adds 10% for no reason.
 You know, when the present value of its future cash flows appreciate by a tenth, sans news.
